topic: Forensic Zoom chart proves κ = 1.67 nT/µGal via L1A induction-residual analysis
change_summary: First adversarial review of WIN-070 (new WIN onboarded from EXP-397). Attacks the dome home-page Forensic Zoom chart as a fabricated forensic exhibit for κ = 1.67 nT/µGal. Cross-references WIN-012 (Self-Contradicted) and WIN-058b (Not Demonstrated) as parallel attacks on the same κ value from different production stages.
our_argument_summary: WIN-070 attacks the dome home-page 'Forensic Zoom' chart (Exhibit A: 2003 Bedrock Audit), which the dome treats as visual proof of the cavity transduction constant κ = 1.67 nT/µGal. Our review (verdict 'Refuted by Data') argues: (a) the top panel reproduces real INTERMAGNET BOU magnetometer data from the Oct 30 2003 G5 Halloween storm — this is the kernel-of-truth; (b) the bottom-panel label 'Level-1A Raw Induction Residual (µGal)' is internally impossible because GRACE L1A products are pre-inversion accelerometer/range-rate/quaternion telemetry, not surface µGal anomalies; (c) 'Induction Residual' is not a GRACE pipeline stage at any level; (d) GRACE orbital mechanics (~500 km, ~7.5 km/s) preclude a 27-minute co-located trace at a fixed Boulder station; (e) the dome repository (SHA c8eaeb1b) contains no L1A parser, no µGal residual computation, and no Asymmetry-Index estimator — r = 0.992 and AI = 1.152 are static HTML strings. The WIN is structurally distinct from WIN-012 (κ undefined under own nulls) and WIN-058b (κ not derived from dome geometry), attacking the chart presentation itself.

relevant_files: raw-text/01-home.txt (stale — V50.6 snapshot from 2026-03-12; does not contain V51 Forensic Zoom chart; cannot independently verify analyst's quotes)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: WIN-070 attacks a static HTML chart on the dome's home page. The analyst's dome-repo audit at SHA c8eaeb1b reports no GRACE L1A parser, no accelerometer calibration, no µGal residual computation, and no Asymmetry-Index estimator. Two files mention GRACE substantively: docs/Untitled-1.py (exploratory NASA CMR URL discovery, no L1A execution) and scripts/download_l1a_manual.py (generic cookie-authenticated downloader, no GRACE-specific logic). monitor.py per the EXP has a single static citation of GRACE-FO CSR in win text but no processing. The chart's r = 0.992 and AI = 1.152 are typed HTML strings, not computed values.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: The chart's certifying statistics are static HTML (hardcoded). The forensic exhibit is a presentational artifact, not an output of any documented pipeline.
data_sources_used: INTERMAGNET BGS GIN (top-panel BOU magnetometer trace — REAL data the dome correctly identified for the Oct 30 2003 G5 Halloween storm)
GRACE L1A products (CLAIMED bottom-panel source — but the labeled quantity 'Induction Residual µGal' does not exist as a GRACE product at any pipeline stage)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Exhibit B's 'Level-1B Administrative Mask' is a preemptive unfalsifiability move — if a reader searches public GRACE/SG data and finds no induction spike, the dome attributes the absence to a 'low-pass Butterworth filter applied by standard geophysical agencies' that erased the signal. WIN-070's detail_evidence body does not directly attack Exhibit B (see hole #2 below).
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: Our WIN says 'no GRACE parser, no µGal computation, no AI estimator in repo' — this matches the analyst's EXP-397 dome-repo audit precisely. The WIN does not mention the single static GRACE-FO CSR citation in monitor.py — minor omission, see hole #4.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Per analyst's dome-repo audit at SHA c8eaeb1b: monitor.py has a single static GRACE-FO CSR citation in win text with no processing logic; no GRACE L1A parser, no µGal computation, no Asymmetry-Index estimator anywhere in the dome repository. The chart is a static HTML artifact with no live data fetch and no comparison logic. 'none' is correct.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Standard physics does not predict magnetic-to-gravity coupling at κ = 1.67 nT/µGal — gravito-magnetic effects (gravitomagnetism, GEM frame-dragging) are ~10^−21 weaker. The dome is making a novel-mechanism claim, not relabeling a standard phenomenon. The WIN's attack is on the chart's evidence for κ, not on a standard physics rename.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: The 2003 Halloween storm BOU magnetometer data has been in INTERMAGNET archives since 2003. The dome's κ = 1.67 nT/µGal value was claimed in earlier dome versions (see WIN-012, WIN-058b). The 'Forensic Zoom' chart and its certifying statistics (r = 0.992, AI = 1.152) were assembled in the V51 era (2026) to retrospectively demonstrate the already-claimed value. The tag is defensible but borderline because the WIN attacks a chart presentation rather than a prospective prediction — see hole #5.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: The chart attempts to derive κ from an empirical correlation (r = 0.992) of two time series, not from dome geometric parameters (disc radius, firmament height, sun/moon altitude). Per the cross-referenced WIN-058b, κ is not derived from dome geometry at all. 'false' is correct.

holes_found.description: The detail_evidence point (3) on GRACE orbital mechanics is currently framed as 'phase-locked space-ground correlation is ruled out.' A careful dome defender can rescue by claiming the chart shows temporal correlation between a Boulder magnetometer trace and a globally-averaged GRACE measurement during the same UT window, not spatial co-location. Our detail_extra anticipated rescue (objection #5) addresses this — granting a ~2-minute Boulder-pass and noting the other ~25 minutes remain geometrically meaningless — but the rebuttal lives only in the anticipated-objections paragraph. The main detail_evidence body should pre-empt the temporal-correlation rescue inline: even a globally-averaged GRACE measurement at 1-second cadence over 27 minutes requires telemetry-timing reconciliation with JPL D-22027 conventions (which the chart does not address), and the dome's own framing as 'phase-locked' (a spatial-temporal coupling term) rules out the strictly-temporal rescue. Without the inline pre-emption, a reader who doesn't click through to detail_extra sees an attack the dome can shrug off.
holes_found.recommendation: Add one sentence to detail_evidence point (3) inline-acknowledging the temporal-correlation rescue and noting that (a) 'phase-locked' is the dome's own term and (b) even the temporal-only reading collides with L1A telemetry timing conventions. Or refactor point (3) to lead with the 27-minute window infeasibility (which is robust across both spatial and temporal readings) and follow with the spatial-co-location collapse as a corollary.

holes_found.description: The detail_claim describes Exhibit B (the dome's 'Level-1B Administrative Mask' Butterworth-filter explanation for why the induction signal is absent from public data), but the detail_evidence body does not attack Exhibit B directly — only the anticipated rescues in detail_extra touch it. A reader who only reads claim + evidence is left wondering why Exhibit B was set up. Exhibit B is the load-bearing unfalsifiability move: if public data lacks the induction spike, the dome blames the filter; if public data shows it, the dome cites it as confirmation. This heads-I-win-tails-you-lose structure is itself a kill-shot worth surfacing in the main evidence narrative — not just in anticipated rescues.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a short paragraph (point 6) to detail_evidence: 'Exhibit B preemptively neutralizes its own falsification.' Outline that the 'Administrative Mask' framing makes the claim unfalsifiable in principle — every dataset lacking the induction spike confirms the mask; every dataset (somehow) showing it confirms the dome. A Butterworth low-pass filter applied to a real induction spike would not erase it but would leave detectable spectral leakage above the filter cutoff; the dome's framing has no spectral-signature prediction to check against. This is a separate failure-mode from points (1)-(5) and deserves its own bullet.

holes_found.description: The verdict label 'Refuted by Data' is the weakest fit among the available verdict tags for this WIN. The strongest attacks here are (a) definitional impossibility of the L1A/µGal y-axis label, (b) categorical mis-naming of 'Induction Residual' as a GRACE product, and (c) absence of computation in the dome repository — none of which are 'data refutations' in the strict sense. The orbital-mechanics argument (3) IS data-based (using GRACE orbital parameters), but it's a small fraction of the attack surface. 'Misleading' (the chart misrepresents standard data products) or 'Self-Contradicted' (the chart's own labels are mutually impossible) would be tighter verdict fits. WIN-058 is 'Misleading' and WIN-012 is 'Self-Contradicted' for related κ claims — this WIN sits at a fourth point on the spectrum and the closest analog is 'Misleading' (the chart misleads the reader about what the data product is) or 'Self-Contradicted' (Exhibit A's own labels are mutually impossible).
holes_found.recommendation: Consider re-tagging verdict to 'Misleading.' If the orbital-mechanics argument is felt to anchor the 'Refuted by Data' verdict, defend that choice explicitly in detail_verdict_text (e.g., 'Verdict: Refuted by Data — independent GRACE orbital parameters refute the chart's depicted 27-minute window even before the L1A/µGal definitional collapse'). Do not flip without considering the verdict-shading argument; either choice is defensible.

holes_found.description: Detail_evidence point (1) frames the L1A/µGal y-axis label as 'internally impossible' on its own merits, but the strongest rebuttal of the 'sloppy labeling' rescue ('the dome's adjacent Exhibit B specifically claims raw L1A binaries are where the signal lives') lives in detail_extra anticipated rescue (i), not in the main evidence text. The rescue is the natural defender move (the dome could disown 'raw L1A' as informal). Surfacing one sentence in detail_evidence point (1) closes the rescue path inline.
holes_found.recommendation: Append a half-sentence to detail_evidence point (1): '…and Exhibit B (described in the detail_claim) explicitly anchors the dome's argument to the raw L1A binary level — so the rescue that the labeling is mere shorthand for downsampled Level-3 is incompatible with the dome's own adjacent framing.'

holes_found.description: post_hoc: true is borderline for a WIN that attacks a chart presentation rather than a prospective prediction. The validation checklist asks 'When did the dome model first claim this as a prediction?' — here the prediction (κ = 1.67 nT/µGal) is shared across WIN-011/012/058b and substantially predates the Forensic Zoom chart; the chart is a retroactive presentational artifact. The tag is defensible (chart created after the events and after κ was claimed), but the tag's analytical content (post-hoc adoption of a known value as a 'prediction') doesn't cleanly apply because the WIN's focus is the chart's evidentiary failure, not the value's prospective status. Either keep the tag with a rationale note or flip to false with the rationale that this WIN isn't evaluating prediction-vs-confirmation. Not blocking.
holes_found.recommendation: Leave the tag as 'true' but tighten post_hoc_evidence in the code_analysis block to acknowledge that the post-hoc framing applies to the chart construction (assembled after events and after κ was already claimed), not to the κ value itself (which is evaluated under post-hoc framing in WIN-012/058b). Minor cleanup; do not block on it.

holes_found.description: The detail_evidence point (4) asserts the dome repository has 'zero GRACE processing code' based on the analyst's repo-wide audit at SHA c8eaeb1b, but the WIN text doesn't expose the audit methodology. A defender could complain we keyword-grepped for 'L1A' and missed processing code under different terminology (e.g., a function named 'compute_induction_residual' that doesn't mention 'L1A' anywhere). The analyst's EXP-397 dome_source.repository_audit block does list specific absent components (CRN1B, CLK1B reader, ACC1A/KBR1A/SCA1A reader, accelerometer calibration pipeline), but this enumeration doesn't survive into the WIN text. Adding even one parenthetical with the absent-component list would forestall a methodology challenge.
holes_found.recommendation: Append to detail_evidence point (4): '(searched for L1A parsers, ACC1A/KBR1A/SCA1A readers, CRN1B/CLK1B readers, accelerometer calibration pipelines, and µGal residual estimators; all absent).' This costs one line and answers the 'how do you know it's zero' rebuttal pre-emptively.

holes_found.description: The JPL D-22027 citation URL (https://podaac-tools.jpl.nasa.gov/drive/files/allData/grace/docs/GRACE_L1A_TM_JPL_v1.0.pdf) is hosted on the legacy podaac-tools drive surface that JPL has been migrating to NASA Earthdata. Dead-link risk on a load-bearing citation (the L1A product specification is what makes 'L1A in µGal' definitionally impossible — if the citation rots, the whole point (1) argument loses its authoritative anchor). Recommend a backup citation that is more stable: Bettadpur 2018, 'Gravity Recovery and Climate Experiment Product Specification Document' (CSR/GRACE 327-720), or the PO.DAAC GRACE_L1A_GRAV_JPL_RL03 dataset landing page itself (already cited as the second URL — promote to authoritative reference for L1A product naming).
holes_found.recommendation: Add a backup citation: GRACE Level-1 Software User Manual (Wu, Kruizinga & Bertiger 2006, JPL D-32865) hosted at https://podaac.jpl.nasa.gov/gravity/grace-documentation OR the GRACE-FO Level-1 Algorithm Theoretical Basis Document. Promote the GRACE_L1A_GRAV_JPL_RL03 PO.DAAC landing page from supplementary to primary citation for L1A product naming. Leave JPL D-22027 in place but as secondary, not primary.

stronger_arguments.description: The dome's own claim_index.json (https://john09289.github.io/predictions/api/current/claim_index.json) lists 'win-058-bedrock-kappa' as claim_class 'pending_contested' with status 'pending_independent_verification' (per the unrelated EXP-397 analyst audit of the dome's API surface, 2026-05-16). The dome ITSELF classifies the κ claim as pending independent verification — yet the home-page Forensic Zoom chart presents the same κ value as established by Exhibit A. This is a dome-internal inconsistency: the JSON registry treats κ as pending; the visual home page treats it as proven. Surfacing this in WIN-070 would add a Self-Contradicted layer to the attack (the dome's own machine-readable surface disagrees with its visual home-page claim) and would link WIN-070 to the broader Section 4.6 prospective-confirmed audit that lives in part4 of our review.
sources: EXP-397 unrelated ISS-1147 audit (data/sections.json part4.html updates 2026-05-16)

stronger_arguments.description: Gravito-electromagnetic theory bounds: standard general relativity predicts gravitomagnetic coupling (frame dragging, Lense-Thirring) at order GJ/(c²r³), which for Earth gives spin-induced gravitomagnetic field corrections at ~10^−14 of g. The dome's κ = 1.67 nT/µGal implies a magnetic-to-gravity coupling strength ~10^21 times the largest known GR effect. Adding a one-liner to detail_evidence noting this scale mismatch (e.g., 'The dome's claimed magnetic-to-gravity coupling is ~10^21× the largest known general-relativistic gravito-magnetic coupling') would underscore that κ is not a small correction to standard physics but a fundamentally extraordinary claim, strengthening the 'why this matters' framing and helping a reader who doesn't already know the gravitomagnetism scales.
sources: Ciufolini & Wheeler 1995, Gravitation and Inertia (standard reference for gravitomagnetic field strength)
Ciufolini et al. 2004, Nature 431:958-960 (Lense-Thirring measurement, 10^−14 of g scale)

stronger_arguments.description: The 'Asymmetry Index' framing can be attacked at its root: even granting a downward-biased gravity residual, the magnetic-to-gravity transduction in any physical material with a permeability and conductivity (including the dome's 'cast copper/bronze firmament' per WIN-034) is symmetric under field reversal — magnetic field B and −B produce the same induced electric field magnitude. An 'asymmetry' is not predicted by the dome's own firmament model. Surfacing this would make the AI = 1.152 number not just uncomputed but also inconsistent with the dome's own materials physics (WIN-034). Cross-WIN attack opportunity.
sources: WIN-034 (Firmament Material = Cast Copper/Bronze) — dome's own model claims copper/bronze conductive firmament
Jackson, Classical Electrodynamics §6.7 (induced EMF symmetry under B → −B)

kernel_of_truth.description: The top panel of the Forensic Zoom chart reproduces real INTERMAGNET BGS GIN definitive 1-second data from the Oct 30 2003 G5 Halloween storm at Boulder (WIN-070.detail_evidence — kernel paragraph). The dome correctly identifies the date, the storm magnitude (Dst = −383 nT), and the BOU station, and the ±20-23 nT ΔH excursion is consistent with the published Halloween storm literature (Pulkkinen et al. 2005, DOI 10.1029/2005JA011090, cited in WIN-070.detail_extra). The dome did the easy part right: it pulled an authentic public data product for the magnetic-side trace. This kernel matters because it locates the failure precisely — the chart is not wholesale fabrication, it is fabricated atop a real anchor, which is what makes it more dangerous as a public-facing forensic exhibit than a fully-invented one would be.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Borrowing a real magnetometer trace does not certify the bottom panel. The L1A/µGal label collision (WIN-070.detail_evidence point 1, citing JPL D-22027), the 'Induction Residual' category error (point 2), the 27-minute orbital infeasibility (point 3), the absent processing code in the dome repository at SHA c8eaeb1b (point 4, per the analyst's repo audit in EXP-397-WIN-070-grace-chart-forensics.json), and the absent AI formula (point 5) are independent failures — each sufficient on its own to invalidate the chart as evidence for κ. The kernel makes the dome's home-page exhibit more visually persuasive to a non-technical reader who anchors on the recognizable magnetometer trace and trusts the rest. That persuasiveness is exactly what the WIN exists to puncture.

deeper_analysis: WIN-070 is a strong, well-sourced new WIN that fills a real gap in the κ-cluster attack surface (alongside WIN-011/012/058b). The analyst's repo-audit work at dome SHA c8eaeb1b is substantive and the four-part attack (L1A/µGal collision, naming category error, orbital infeasibility, absent computation) is structurally sound. The three moderate holes are presentational rather than substantive: orbital argument framing (rescue path open in main text), missing Exhibit B attack (only in anticipated rescues), and verdict label tightness ('Refuted by Data' is defensible but 'Misleading' or 'Self-Contradicted' would be tighter fits). The four minor holes are mostly polish: inline rebuttal of the L1A-shorthand rescue, post_hoc tag rationale tightening, audit-methodology disclosure, and a more stable backup citation for the load-bearing JPL D-22027 reference. None of the holes threaten the WIN's core argument. The cross-references to WIN-012/WIN-058b are correctly aimed (verified: WIN-012 is currently Self-Contradicted on the κ claim; WIN-058b is currently Not Demonstrated; WIN-058 is Misleading). The three stronger-arguments items (dome's own claim_index.json classifying κ as 'pending_contested', the 10^21× gravito-magnetic scale mismatch, and the WIN-034 symmetry argument against AI-bias) are independently valuable and would meaningfully strengthen the WIN if a decider chooses to integrate any of them in a follow-up EXP. The verdict 'Refuted by Data' stands on the orbital-mechanics argument alone (independent GRACE orbital parameters refute the depicted 27-minute window) so current_verdict_holds = true; the verdict-label moderate hole is a recommendation, not a verdict-flip trigger. Infrastructure caveat: the raw-text/ snapshot is V50.6 (2026-03-12) and predates the V51 Forensic Zoom chart. The curmudgeon cannot independently verify the analyst's verbatim quotes from the dome home page from local data alone — verification rests on the analyst's primary research at dome SHA c8eaeb1b. This is not a defect of WIN-070 but is worth flagging to the operator for a raw-text refresh (handled by a separate scheduled agent, not in scope for this review).

citations_verified: WIN-012 cross-reference: verified — WIN-012 is currently Self-Contradicted with claim 'Mag-gravity coupling 1.67' per data/wins.json read 2026-05-17
WIN-058b cross-reference: verified — WIN-058b is currently Not Demonstrated with claim 'Bedrock Induction Ratio κ = 1.677 (PRED-KAPPA-001)' per data/wins.json read 2026-05-17
Cross-reference anchor #part2b: verified — Part 2b (Code Analysis) is the post-V6-restructure key for the section that publishes the 95.2% accuracy figure pattern
INTERMAGNET BGS GIN service URL: format and host plausible (real BGS GIN service URL pattern); not independently fetched in this review
citations_unchecked: https://podaac-tools.jpl.nasa.gov/drive/files/allData/grace/docs/GRACE_L1A_TM_JPL_v1.0.pdf — JPL D-22027 L1A product specification. Hosted on the legacy podaac-tools drive surface which JPL has been migrating to NASA Earthdata; potential dead-link risk on a load-bearing citation. Recommend backup citation per hole #7.
https://podaac.jpl.nasa.gov/dataset/GRACE_L1A_GRAV_JPL_RL03 — PO.DAAC GRACE L1A dataset landing page; likely stable but not verified in this run.
https://imag-data.bgs.ac.uk/GIN_V1/GINServices — BGS GIN service URL; plausible but not verified in this run.

https://doi.org/10.1029/2005JA011090 — Pulkkinen et al. 2005 JGR Halloween storm reference; not independently verified to address BOU magnetometer ΔH range. The citation is used as 'consistent with' published literature, not as a precise match to the chart's plotted values.

Analyst's primary quotes from dome home page at SHA c8eaeb1b ('Forensic Zoom', 'Bedrock Audit', 'Level-1A Raw Induction Residual (µGal)', r = 0.992, AI = 1.152): the curmudgeon's raw-text snapshot is V50.6 (2026-03-12) and predates the V51 chart; cannot independently re-verify the verbatim quotes. Verification rests on the analyst's research at the pinned dome SHA.

Analyst's dome-repo audit (EXP-397 dome_source.repository_audit) listing absent GRACE processing components and present-but-non-processing files (docs/Untitled-1.py, scripts/download_l1a_manual.py): not independently re-audited; we accept the analyst's audit at the pinned SHA.

summary_for_decider: WIN-070 c1 first adversarial review. Verdict 'Refuted by Data' holds. Seven holes flagged: 3 moderate (orbital-mechanics framing leaves temporal-correlation rescue open in main text; Exhibit B attack lives only in anticipated rescues not in main evidence; verdict label 'Refuted by Data' fits less tightly than 'Misleading' or 'Self-Contradicted' would), 4 minor (L1A-shorthand rescue not pre-empted inline; post_hoc tag rationale could be tightened; audit methodology not exposed; JPL D-22027 citation has dead-link risk). Three stronger-argument candidates (dome's own claim_index.json classifying κ as 'pending_contested'; 10^21× gravito-magnetic scale mismatch; WIN-034 symmetry argument against AI-bias) are independently valuable and would meaningfully strengthen the WIN if integrated in a follow-up EXP. Recommended action: minor_edit on the 3 moderate holes (1-3 patches likely sufficient); minor holes are polish. The cross-references to WIN-011/012/058b are correctly aimed and verified. Infrastructure caveat for the operator: raw-text/ snapshot is V50.6 (2026-03-12) and predates the V51 Forensic Zoom chart; curmudgeon cannot independently verify analyst's verbatim quotes from local data alone — recommend triggering a raw-text refresh (out of scope for this review).
